title: portulaca oleracea subsp. macrantha
intro: Portulaca oleracea subsp. macrantha, also known as the Indian purslane, is a subspecies of the Portulaca oleracea plant, which is a succulent herb with small, fleshy leaves and stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Indian purslane include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Indian purslane can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
